question: Point A is at (2, 3). It is translated 4 units right and 1 unit up. What are the new coordinates?
hints: Moving right adds to x. Moving up adds to y.

question: Point B is at (5, 2). It is reflected over the x-axis. What are the new coordinates?
hints: Reflecting over the x-axis changes the sign of the y-coordinate.

question: Point C is at (3, -1). It is reflected over the y-axis. What are the new coordinates?
hints: Reflecting over the y-axis changes the sign of the x-coordinate.

question: Point D is at (1, 4). It is rotated 90° clockwise about the origin. What are the new coordinates?
hints: A 90° clockwise rotation maps (x, y) to (y, −x).

question: Triangle PQR has vertices P(1, 2), Q(4, 2), and R(4, 5). It is translated 3 units left and 2 units down. What are the coordinates of the translated vertices?
hints: Subtract 3 from each x-coordinate and 2 from each y-coordinate.
Apply the same translation to every vertex.
